case_title: RONALD RODRIGUEZ VS. RETIREMENT BOARD OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN
cause_of_action: WRITS OF MANDATE OR PROH., CERTI., ETC./ADMIN. AGEN

docket_entries.description: LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER RONALD RODRIGUEZ MOTION FOR WRIT OF MANDAMUS PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 1094.5. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - DENIED. THE COURT USES ITS INDEPENDENT JUDGMENT IN REVIEWING THE ADMINISTRATIVE DECISION. THE COURT HAS REVIEWED THE ENTIRE RECORD AND FINDS THAT BASED ON ITS INDEPENDENT JUDGMENT, THE DECISION OF THE HEARING OFFICER IS SUPPORTED BY THE WEIGHT OF THE EVIDENCE. NO MEDICAL EVIDENCE SUPPORTS PETITIONER'S CLAIM THAT HE WAS UNABLE TO PERFORM HIS DUTIES AS AN ACTING BATTALION CHIEF. DR. MANACE FOUND THAT PETITIONER EXPERIENCED NO SIGNIFICANT IMPAIRMENT IN HIS JOB PERFORMANCE AS A FIREFIGHTER AS A RESULT OF HIS HEARING LOSS. (AR 63) HE FURTHER NOTED THAT "PROVIDED [PETITIONER] MAKES REGULAR USE OF APPROVED EAR PROTECTION WHENEVER EXPOSED TO HIGH-INTENSITY OCCUPATIONAL NOISE, THERE ARE NO RESTRICTIONS TO HIS EMPLOYABILITY. WERE HE NOT RETIRED, BASED ON THE ADEQUATE AUDITORY RESERVE, [PETITIONER] COULD RESUME HIS PRIOR JOB CLASSIFICATION AS A FIREFIGHTER WITHOUT RESTRICTION." (AR 67) DR. SCHINDLER NOTED A NONRATABLE HEARING LOSS AT THE TIME OF PETITIONER'S RETIREMENT. (AR 57.) THE RECORD SUPPORTS A FINDING OF AN INDUSTRIAL - CAUSED HEARING LOSS, BUT NOTHING IN THE RECORD SUPPORTS PETITIONER'S CONTENTION THAT HE WAS DISABLED FROM PERFORMING HIS USUAL DUTIES UNTIL HE CHOSE TO RETIRE. HE ACTIVELY PERFORMED HIS DUTIES AS AN ACTING BATTALION CHIEF THROUGH THE DATE OF HIS RETIREMENT, AND NO PHYSICIAN CONSIDERED HIS HEARING LOSS TO HAVE RENDERED HIM UNABLE TO PERFORM THOSE DUTIES. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Ntc And Motion For Writ Of Mandamus Pursuant To Ccp 1094.5
Off Calendar Sep-30-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
PETITIONER RONALD RODRIGUEZ MOTION FOR WRIT OF MANDAMUS PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 1094.5.

APPEARANCES: Michael S. Hebel, Esq. of San Francisco Police Officers' Association 861-0211 for petitioner & moving party; Rebecca Liu, Deputy City Atty 554-3870 for respondent & opposing party.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - DENIED. THE COURT USES ITS INDEPENDENT JUDGMENT IN REVIEWING THE ADMINISTRATIVE DECISION. THE COURT HAS REVIEWED THE ENTIRE RECORD AND FINDS THAT BASED ON ITS INDEPENDENT JUDGMENT, THE DECISION OF THE HEARING OFFICER IS SUPPORTED BY THE WEIGHT OF THE EVIDENCE. NO MEDICAL EVIDENCE SUPPORTS PETITIONER'S CLAIM THAT HE WAS UNABLE TO PERFORM HIS DUTIES AS AN ACTING BATTALION CHIEF. DR. MANACE FOUND THAT PETITIONER EXPERIENCED NO SIGNIFICANT IMPAIRMENT IN HIS JOB PERFORMANCE AS A FIREFIGHTER AS A RESULT OF HIS HEARING LOSS. (AR 63) HE FURTHER NOTED THAT "PROVIDED [PETITIONER] MAKES REGULAR USE OF APPROVED EAR PROTECTION WHENEVER EXPOSED TO HIGH-INTENSITY OCCUPATIONAL NOISE, THERE ARE NO RESTRICTIONS TO HIS EMPLOYABILITY. WERE HE NOT RETIRED, BASED ON THE ADEQUATE AUDITORY RESERVE, [PETITIONER] COULD RESUME HIS PRIOR JOB CLASSIFICATION AS A FIREFIGHTER WITHOUT RESTRICTION." (AR 67) DR. SCHINDLER NOTED A NONRATABLE HEARING LOSS AT THE TIME OF PETITIONER'S RETIREMENT. (AR 57.) THE RECORD SUPPORTS A FINDING OF AN INDUSTRIAL - CAUSED HEARING LOSS, BUT NOTHING IN THE RECORD SUPPORTS PETITIONER'S CONTENTION THAT HE WAS DISABLED FROM PERFORMING HIS USUAL DUTIES UNTIL HE CHOSE TO RETIRE. HE ACTIVELY PERFORMED HIS DUTIES AS AN ACTING BATTALION CHIEF THROUGH THE DATE OF HIS RETIREMENT, AND NO PHYSICIAN CONSIDERED HIS HEARING LOSS TO HAVE RENDERED HIM UNABLE TO PERFORM THOSE DUTIES. Order signed in open court. (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
